# FitOps-CLI

> FitOps is a local-first fitness analytics platform for Python 3.11+. It syncs activities to a personal SQLite database (`~/.fitops/fitops.db`) and exposes the data through two first-class interfaces: a browser dashboard at `localhost:8888` and a structured CLI designed for AI agent consumption. Every CLI command outputs either Rich-formatted tables for humans or JSON with `_meta` context blocks and `data_availability` hints for agents. No cloud, no subscriptions — your data never leaves your machine.

Install with `pip install fitops-cli` or run without installing via `uvx fitops`. Requires a Strava API application (Client ID + Secret) for authentication. A Claude Code skill is available for AI agent use: download `.claude/commands/fitops.md` from the repository and place it in your project's `.claude/commands/` directory.

## Getting Started

- [README](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/README.md): Full project overview, installation options (uvx / pip / source), quick start, and command reference.
- [Getting Started Guide](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/getting-started/README.md): Step-by-step walkthrough from Strava app creation through first sync.
- [Comparison with TrainingPeaks, Intervals.icu, Strava, Garmin Connect](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/comparison.md): Feature-by-feature breakdown with sources from community forums.

## CLI Command Reference

- [Auth commands](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/commands/auth.md): `fitops auth login/logout/status/refresh` — Strava OAuth with local callback server on port 8080.
- [Sync commands](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/commands/sync.md): `fitops sync run [--full] [--after DATE]` / `fitops sync streams` / `fitops sync status` — incremental and full historical sync with 3-day overlap.
- [Activities commands](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/commands/activities.md): `fitops activities list/get/streams/laps` — list, filter, and retrieve time-series GPS/HR/power streams.
- [Athlete commands](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/commands/athlete.md): `fitops athlete profile/stats/zones/set/equipment` — profile, cumulative stats, HR/power zones, physiology settings (max HR, FTP, LTHR).
- [Analytics commands](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/commands/analytics.md): `fitops analytics training-load/vo2max/zones/trends/performance/power-curve/pace-zones/snapshot` — CTL/ATL/TSB, VO2max estimates, LT1/LT2 thresholds, running economy.
- [Workouts commands](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/commands/workouts.md): `fitops workouts list/show/create/link/compliance/simulate` — Markdown workout files with YAML frontmatter, per-segment HR/pace compliance scoring, terrain+weather simulation on imported courses.
- [Race commands](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/commands/README.md): `fitops race import/courses/course/simulate/splits` — import GPX/TCX/Strava courses, per-km split planning adjusted for grade, temperature, humidity, and wind. Supports pacer strategy (sit-then-push) and race-day forecast weather.
- [Weather commands](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/commands/weather.md): `fitops weather fetch/show/forecast/set` — historical and forecast weather via Open-Meteo (no API key), WBGT heat stress index, weather-adjusted pace (WAP), wind modeling via Pugh 1971.
- [Notes commands](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/commands/README.md): `fitops notes create/list/get/edit/delete/tags/sync` — Markdown training notes stored in `~/.fitops/notes/`, tagged and optionally linked to activities. Serves as persistent memory for AI agents across sessions.
- [Dashboard command](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/commands/README.md): `fitops dashboard serve [--port N]` — starts the local FastAPI dashboard at `http://localhost:8888`.

## Key Concepts

- [Output format and JSON schema](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/concepts/llm-integration.md): Every JSON response has a `_meta` block (tool, version, timestamp, filters) and `data_availability` per activity so an agent knows what to fetch next. Units are explicit in field names (`_m`, `_km`, `_s`, `_bpm`). Paces are formatted as `"6:03"` strings alongside raw m/s.
- [Storage layout](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/concepts/README.md): `~/.fitops/config.json` (credentials + physiology), `~/.fitops/sync_state.json`, `~/.fitops/fitops.db` (SQLite), `~/.fitops/workouts/` (Markdown), `~/.fitops/notes/` (Markdown). Override base dir with `FITOPS_DIR` env var.
- [Training load methodology](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/concepts/training-load.md): CTL = 42-day EWMA (α≈0.0465), ATL = 7-day EWMA (α≈0.25), TSB = CTL − ATL. TSS per activity from pace (running), power (cycling), or HR zone multipliers (fallback).
- [VO2max methodology](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/concepts/vo2max.md): Weighted composite of VDOT/Daniels (50%), McArdle (30%), and Costill (20–40%). Age-adjusted with 0.8%/yr decline factor above age 25. Heat-corrected using Nybo/González-Alonso model.
- [Weather-adjusted pace](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/concepts/weather-pace.md): WBGT-based temperature/humidity model (Ely et al. 2007) + wind drag model (Pugh 1971). True Pace = actual pace normalized for both elevation (GAP) and weather (WAP).
- [Zone methodology](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/concepts/zones.md): Three methods — LTHR (5-zone, best accuracy), Max HR percentage, HRR/Karvonen. Auto-selection based on available physiology data. LT1/LT2 explicitly calculated.
- [Workout file format](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/concepts/README.md): Markdown files in `~/.fitops/workouts/` with YAML frontmatter. Segments defined as time-in-zone, pace-range, or HR-range steps. Compliance score 0.0–1.0 against actual HR stream; ≥0.8 = successful.
- [Notes as agent memory](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/concepts/llm-integration.md): Notes in `~/.fitops/notes/` are plain Markdown with YAML frontmatter (title, tags, activity_id). An AI agent can write observations after analysis and read them back in future sessions. Tags enable structured recall (e.g. `fitops notes list --tag pattern`).

## Output Examples

- [JSON output examples](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/customerfacing/output-examples/README.md): Sample responses for activities list, activity detail, training load snapshot, race simulation splits, weather-adjusted pace, and streams data.

## Optional

- [Changelog](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): Version history and release notes.
- [Development setup](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/README.md): `pip install -e ".[dev]"` and `pytest tests/ -v`.
- [Roadmap](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/roadmap.md): Planned phases — multi-provider sync (Garmin, Apple Health, Coros), cloud backup (Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox).
- [Architecture](https://github.com/yourname/FitOps-CLI/blob/main/docs/architecture.md): Module layout, async design, database schema, and the Duality Rule (every feature must be accessible in both CLI and dashboard).
